page: Amazon Web Services c8in.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c8in family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor running at a dedicated clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 virtual CPUs (vCPUs), which map to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation.

This instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and is optimized for EBS and network performance.

The hardware profile makes this instance suitable for compute-bound workloads that require dedicated CPU resources and high clock speeds but do not demand large memory footprints or local storage. Typical workloads include batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel processor running at 3.9 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not contain local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This resource configuration is designed for compute-intensive workloads, such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics, where high clock speeds and dedicated CPU performance are prioritized over large memory capacities.
